subcodes.title: Words spoken at Hearst estate in Pleasanton, 1912-10-14
subcodes.first_line: The president must be a man who does not insistently seek the presidency. He should be a person free from all thoughts of name and rank; rather, he should say, 'I am unworthy and incapable of this position
subcodes.first_line_orig: باید شخصی رئیس باشد که خود اصرار در ریاست نداشته باشد فی الحقیقه در خیال شهرت و برتری نباشد
subcodes.subjects: @elections, @serving humanity; working for progress, betterment of the world, @humility; meekness; lowliness, @selfishness; self-love; egotism; self-indulgence
